title: Sharp non-uniqueness for the Navier-Stokes equations in R^3

def:1.1.summary: Very weak solution of the Navier-Stokes equations on $R^3$: u in $L^2([0,T]$ x $R^3)$ is divergence-free a.e. and satisfies the integral identity against test functions in $D_T$.

thm:1.2.summary: Sharp and strong non-uniqueness: for $1 \le$ p < 2, any weak solution u of the Navier-Stokes equations in $L^p([0,T_0]$; $L^\infty(R^3))$ is non-unique. Sharp at the Ladyzhenskaya-Prodi-Serrin endpoint $(2,\infty)$.

rem:1.3.summary: Compares with Cheskidov-Luo's $T^3$ result which required at least one regularity interval. This result removes that assumption and works on the whole space $R^3$ via an incompressible perturbation fluid technique.

lem:2.1.summary: Geometric Lemma: decomposes any symmetric matrix R near Id as a sum of rank-one tensors sum $a_k^2(R)$ kbar tensor kbar over directions k in $\Lambda$ subset $S^2$ cap $Q^3$.

rem:2.2.summary: Explicit construction of $\Lambda$ with 6 rational directions in $S^2$ cap $Q^3$ and the associated orthonormal bases (k, kbar, kbarbar).

lem:2.3.summary: Decoupling estimate: for smooth f supported on [-L/2,L/2]^d and periodic g on $T^d, ||fg(\lambda$ .)||_Lp ~ ||f||_Lp ||g||_Lp up to an error of order $\lambda^{-1/p}$.

prop:2.4.summary: Gluing of weak solutions: if $u_1$ and $u_2$ are weak solutions on consecutive time intervals with matching data at the junction, the concatenation is a weak solution on the combined interval.

def:2.5.summary: Standard spatial and temporal mollifiers $\psi_\epsilon$ and $\phi_\epsilon$ defined from smooth compactly supported kernels.

def:2.6.summary: Mixed time-spatial Besov spaces $tilde{L}^r_T B^s_{p,q}(R^d)$ defined via Littlewood-Paley decomposition.

lem:2.7.summary: Maximal regularity for the heat equation in Besov spaces: if $u_0$ in $B^s_{p,q}$ and f in $tilde{L}^{r_2}_t B^{s-2+2/r_2}_{p,q}$, then u in $tilde{L}^{r_1}_t B^{s+2/r_1}_{p,q}$ with universal constant.

prop:3.1.summary: Main iterative proposition: given $(u_q, p_q$, $Rring_q)$ solving the Navier-Stokes-Reynolds system at level q, constructs $(u_{q+1}, p_{q+1}$, $Rring_{q+1})$ at level q+1 satisfying all inductive estimates, with $u_{q+1}=u_q$ on [0, $T/4+4\lambda_q^{-1}]$ and $||u_{q+1}-u_q|| \le C_0 \delta_{q+1}^{1/2}$.

prop:4.1.summary: Mollification estimates: bounds on derivatives of $(u_{ell_q}$, $Rring_{ell_q}, R^rem_q)$ in Sobolev norms, with $||Rring_{ell_q}||_{L^1_{t,x}} \le \delta_{q+1} \lambda_q^{-4\alpha}$.

prop:4.2.summary: Fourier coefficient estimates for the intermittent shear function $\phi_r$: $|c_{m,r}| \le CK^{9/2} r^{-9/2} m^{-4}$, giving rapid decay in the frequency parameter m.

prop:4.3.summary: Estimates for the building blocks: $L^p$ bounds and derivative bounds on the spatial profile $\phi_{(\epsilon_0,1-\epsilon_0,k)}$, temporal concentration $g_{(2,\sigma_0,k)}$, and corrector profile $\psi'$.

prop:4.4.summary: Temporal decomposition: writes $d_t(w^(p)_{q+1}+w^(c)_{q+1})$ as div Rring^(1)_{q+1} + div Rring^(2)_{q+1} + F^(1)_{q+1} + nabla P^(1)_{q+1}, isolating the Reynolds stress contributions from time derivatives.

prop:4.5.summary: Quadratic decomposition: writes div(w^(p)_{q+1} tensor w^(p)_{q+1}) + div $Rring_{ell_q} =$ div $Rring^(3)_{q+1} + F^(2)_{q+1} + F^(3)_{q+1} + nabla(\eta^2_q (\chi_q \rho_q)^{1/2})$. The oscillation error Rring^(3) comes from non-resonant Fourier interactions.

prop:4.6.summary: Estimates for $(\chi_q \rho_q)^{1/2}$ and $(\chi_q \rho_q)^{-1}$ in $L^\infty_t H^N$, including time derivatives. Controls the amplitude function that balances compact support of the Reynolds stress.

prop:4.7.summary: Estimates for the coefficient functions $a_{(k,q)}$ in $L^\infty_t H^N$: $||a_{(k,q)}||_{L^\infty_t H^N} \le C_N ell_q^{-2N}$. These control the amplitude of the intermittent perturbation and appear in every subsequent estimate.

prop:4.8.summary: Estimates for the incompressible perturbation forcing $F_{q+1}$: the Duhamel integral of $F_{q+1}$ in $tilde{L}^\infty_t B^{1/2}_{2,1}$ cap tilde{L}^1_t $B^{5/2}_{2,1}$ is bounded by $\lambda_q^{-20}$. This is the key estimate enabling the incompressible perturbation fluid technique on $R^3$.

prop:4.9.summary: Estimates for the incompressible perturbation w^(t)_{q+1}: $||w^(t)_{q+1}||_{tilde{L}^\infty_t B^{1/2}_{2,1}$ cap tilde{L}^1_t $B^{5/2}_{2,1}} \le (1/2) \delta_{q+2} \lambda_{q+1}^{-6\alpha}$. Obtained via Banach fixed point theorem applied to the mild formulation with forcing $F_{q+1}$.

prop:4.10.summary: Combined perturbation estimates: $||w_{q+1}||_{L^2_{t,x}$ cap $L^p_t L^\infty} \le (1/2) C_0 \delta_{q+1}^{1/2}$ and $||w_{q+1}||_{L^\infty_t H^3} \le (1/2) C_0 \lambda_{q+1}^{9/2} \delta_{q+1}^{1/2}$. Assembles w^(p), w^(c), w^(t) estimates.

prop:4.11.summary: New Reynolds stress estimates: $||Rring_{q+1}||_{L^1_{t,x}} \le \delta_{q+2} \lambda_{q+1}^{-4\alpha}$ and $||Rring_{q+1}||_{L^\infty W^{3,1}} \le \lambda_{q+1}^5$. Closes the inductive bound on the Reynolds stress at level q+1.

prop:4.12.summary: Energy increment estimate: |E $- int_{3T/4}^Tint_{R^3} |u_{q+1}|^2$ dx dt $- 3 \delta_{q+2}| \le \delta_{q+2} \lambda_q^{-\alpha}$. Ensures the prescribed energy profile is maintained at each iteration level.

edges.note: Main theorem stated for weak solutions in the sense of Def 1.1
Proof of Thm 1.2 (Section 3.3) applies Prop 3.1 inductively to build the Cauchy sequence ${u_q}$
Gluing proposition used in Section 3.3 to concatenate u, $u_1$, tilde{v}, V into a single weak solution v
Heat semigroup regularity used in the well-posedness step to get $u_1(t_0+\epsilon)$ in $H^3$
Mollification estimates feed into the perturbation construction
Combined perturbation estimate gives (3.16): $||u_{q+1}-u_q|| \le C_0 \delta_{q+1}^{1/2}$
Reynolds stress bound at q+1 level closes inductive estimate (3.14)
Energy increment estimate closes inductive estimate (3.12)
Incompressible perturbation w^(t) estimate closes inductive estimates (3.10)-(3.11) for nonlocal part
Mollification defined using the mollifiers from Def 2.5
Building block estimates used in the temporal decomposition proof
Coefficient estimates for $a_{(k,q)}$ used to bound Rring^(1), Rring^(2)
Fourier coefficient decay $|c_{m,r}|$ used to bound oscillation error Rring^(3)
Geometric lemma decomposition R = sum $a_k^2$ kbar tensor kbar is the algebraic identity that makes the principal perturbation cancel $Rring_{ell_q}$
Proof uses mollified Reynolds stress bounds from Prop 4.1 to control $\chi_q \rho_q$
Proof combines Prop 4.1 bounds on $Rring_{ell_q}$ with Prop 4.6 bounds on $(\chi_q \rho_q)^{-1}$
$a_{(k,q)} = \eta_q a_k(Id - Rring_{ell_q}/(\chi_q \rho_q)) (\chi_q \rho_q)^{1/2}$, so Prop 4.6 estimates are direct inputs
Heat semigroup estimate in Besov spaces applied to bound the Duhamel integral of $F_{q+1}$
Building block estimates for the spatial profiles and temporal concentrations in F^(1), F^(2), F^(3)
F^(2) defined in Prop 4.5 decomposition; its structure determines the Besov regularity
Coefficient bounds $||a_{(k,q)}||_{W^{3,\infty}}$ used in estimating F^(1), F^(2), F^(3)
F^(1) defined in Prop 4.4 decomposition
Banach fixed point argument uses Prop 4.8 bound ||int $e^{(t-s)\Delta} F_{q+1} ds|| \le \lambda_q^{-20}$ as the forcing estimate
Besov maximal regularity used in the Duhamel formulation of w^(t)_{q+1}
Decoupling lemma used to estimate $||w^(p)_{q+1}||_{L^2_{t,x}}$ and $||w^(c)_{q+1}||_{L^2_{t,x}}$
Building block $L^p$ and $L^\infty$ estimates used for w^(p) and w^(c)
Coefficient bounds on $a_{(k,q)}$ in $L^\infty_t H^N$ control $||w^(p)||_{L^\infty_t H^3}$
w^(t)_{q+1} estimate needed to assemble total $w_{q+1} = w^(p) + w^(c) + w^(t)$
Reynolds stress Rring^(1), Rring^(2) from temporal decomposition estimated here
Reynolds stress Rring^(3) from quadratic decomposition estimated here
Building block estimates used throughout the Reynolds stress bounds
Coefficient estimates on $a_{(k,q)}$ used in bounding all three Reynolds stress components
Fourier coefficient decay used in bounding Rring^(3) via Prop 4.5
w^(t)_{q+1} estimates needed for the transport + Nash error terms in $Rring_{q+1}$
Total perturbation $w_{q+1}$ estimates needed for cross-terms in $Rring_{q+1}$
Mollification estimates used for the barred solution $ubar_q$
w^(t)_{q+1} bounds used to control cross-term III = 2 int $ubar_q$ . $w_{q+1}$
Perturbation $L^2$ estimates needed for term II = int $|w_{q+1}|^2$
Decoupling lemma used to extract the principal energy contribution from $||w^(p)_{q+1}||^2_{L^2}$
The geometric lemma decomposition is the mechanism by which w^(p) tensor w^(p) cancels $Rring_{ell_q}$ at leading order

layer_1_main_result.description: Main theorem reduces to iterating the inductive Prop 3.1, gluing the limit with Prop 2.4

layer_2_preliminaries.description: Geometric lemma, decoupling, gluing, mollifiers, Besov spaces, and heat regularity provide the analytical toolkit

layer_3_induction_framework.description: Induction scheme with parameters $(\lambda_q$, $\delta_q$, $\Omega_q)$ and iterative proposition producing the Cauchy sequence

layer_4_mollification_and_gluing.description: Mollification step produces smooth $(u_{ell_q}$, $Rring_{ell_q}, R^rem_q)$ with quantitative bounds; gluing secures temporal agreement

layer_5_perturbation_construction.description: Intermittent shear flows, Fourier coefficient estimates, building block estimates, and the quadratic/temporal decompositions that define the perturbation

layer_6_amplitude_and_coefficient_estima.description: Controls the amplitude function $(\chi_q \rho_q)$ and coefficient $a_{(k,q)}$ that balance compact support of Reynolds stress with non-compact support of the solution

layer_7_incompressible_perturbation.description: The key new ingredient: incompressible perturbation fluid w^(t)_{q+1} solving a forced Navier-Stokes equation, estimated via Banach fixed point in Besov spaces

layer_8_closing_estimates.description: Assembly of perturbation estimates, Reynolds stress closure, and energy increment that verify all inductive hypotheses at level q+1

1Cheskidov.title: Sharp nonuniqueness for the Navier-Stokes equations
1Cheskidov.note: Invent. Math. 229 (2022). The $T^3$ predecessor; this paper extends the result to $R^3$.

BV.title: Nonuniqueness of weak solutions to the Navier-Stokes equation
BV.note: Ann. of Math. 189 (2019). First convex integration non-uniqueness for Navier-Stokes.

ABC.title: Non-uniqueness of Leray solutions of the forced Navier-Stokes equations
ABC.note: Ann. Math. 196 (2022). Non-uniqueness with external forcing.

2Beekie.title: Weak solutions of ideal MHD which do not conserve magnetic helicity
2Beekie.note: Ann. PDE 6 (2020). Source of the geometric lemma (Lem 2.1) and building block estimates (Prop 4.3).

Ise18.title: A proof of Onsager's conjecture
Ise18.note: Ann. of Math. 188 (2018). Landmark convex integration for Euler equations.

MoS.title: Non-uniqueness for the transport equation with Sobolev vector fields
MoS.note: Ann. PDE 4 (2018). Source of the decoupling lemma (Lem 2.3).

BCD11.title: Fourier Analysis and Nonlinear Partial Differential Equations
BCD11.note: Grundlehren vol. 343 (2011). Source for Besov space theory (Def 2.6, Lem 2.7).

MWZ.title: Littlewood-Paley Theory and Applications to Fluid Dynamics Equations
MWZ.note: Monographs on Modern Pure Mathematics vol. 142 (2012). Co-source for Besov space theory.

I-O.title: On Nonperiodic Euler Flows with Holder Regularity
I-O.note: Arch. Ration. Mech. Anal. 221 (2016). Prior work on extending convex integration from torus to whole space.

ESS.title: L_{3,infty}-solutions to the Navier-Stokes equations and backward uniqueness
ESS.note: Russ. Math. Surv. 58 (2003). Ladyzhenskaya-Prodi-Serrin endpoint uniqueness.

Lady.title: Uniqueness and smoothness of generalized solutions of Navier-Stokes equations
Lady.note: Zap. Naucn. Sem. 5 (1967). Original Ladyzhenskaya uniqueness criterion.

Prodi.title: Un teorema di unicita per le equazioni di Navier-Stokes
Prodi.note: Ann. Mat. Pura Appl. 48 (1959). Original Prodi uniqueness criterion.

Serr.title: On the interior regularity of weak solutions of the Navier-Stokes equations
Serr.note: Arch. Ration. Mech. Anal. 9 (1962). Original Serrin uniqueness criterion.

MY.title: On the weak solutions for the MHD systems with controllable total energy and cross helicity
MY.note: J. Math. Pures Appl. 181 (2024). Prior work by the authors on convex integration for MHD.

MNY.title: On Onsager's type conjecture for the inviscid Boussinesq equations
MNY.note: J. Funct. Anal. 287 (2024). Prior work by the authors on Boussinesq equations.
